name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
fact: The longest-running active lunar orbital mission. LROC's narrow-angle camera resolves objects ≥50 cm — sharp enough to image every Apollo descent stage, both Lunokhod rover tracks, and every recent landed asset. Built the topographic and imagery base maps used by every recent Moon-landing mission. Currently mapping permanently-shadowed craters for Artemis south-pole site selection.
capability: Definitive modern lunar reconnaissance — the eyes-in-the-sky for every recent Moon mission.
